summary: Reordered NewSnapshot (engine/mvcc/read.go) to call em.AcquireCurrentEpoch() BEFORE cat.Get(fileID), closing a TOCTOU race where a concurrent CommitVersion completing between the old order's two steps could cause RunCompaction to prematurely delete a version file still pinned by a live Snapshot. Releases the acquired epoch if the subsequent cat.Get fails, to avoid a refcount leak. Rewrote the previously-inaccurate race-note doc comment with a full proof of race-freedom. Added engine/mvcc/gc_test.go:TestNewSnapshotClosesEpochAcquireVersionReadRace, a hook-based regression test (using a new newSnapshotWithHook test seam in read.go) that pauses NewSnapshot between epoch-acquire and version-read, runs a concurrent CommitVersion + RunCompaction to completion in that window, and asserts the pinned version file survives and reads correctly.
sanity_check: Confirmed the new regression test actually catches the race: temporarily reverted the ordering, observed TestNewSnapshotClosesEpochAcquireVersionReadRace fail with 'version 1 file deleted by RunCompaction while a snapshot was mid-acquire and about to pin to it', then restored the fix and reconfirmed pass.
commands_run: cd engine && go build ./... && go vet ./... -> OK

go test ./mvcc/... -race -v -count=1 -> all PASS (TestEpochRefcount, TestEpochRefcountConcurrent, TestCompactor, TestNewSnapshotClosesEpochAcquireVersionReadRace, TestConcurrentReadersWriters, TestSnapshotRead, TestSnapshotReadNoVersionCommitted, TestVersionWriter, TestCurrentVersionCAS, TestVersionCASWAL)
go test ./... -race -count=1 (from engine/) -> zero regressions across engine, engine/btree, engine/catalog, engine/mvcc, engine/wal
